Lipids
Lipid Panel
Cholesterol H 2.00-5.20 mmol/L 08/Apr/2026 14:245.86
Cholesterol In LDL 1.5-3.4 mmol/L 08/Apr/2026 14:243.3
The optimal LDL cholesterol for intermediate and high risk
individuals is less than 2.1 mmol/L. If triglycerides are
greater than 1.4 mmol/L, consider monitoring of alternate
lipid targets nonHDL cholesterol or apoB. For low risk
individuals with LDL cholesterol greater than 4.9 mmol/L,
target a reduction of LDL cholesterol of at least 50
percent. See Can J Cardiol 2013 vol 29 pgs 151 to 167.
Cholesterol In HDL >1.19 mmol/L 08/Apr/2026 14:241.97

Lipids (continued)
Lipid Panel (continued)
Cholesterol Non HDL <4.3 mmol/L 08/Apr/2026 14:243.9
Non HDL-cholesterol is calculated from total cholesterol and
HDL-C and is not affected by the fasting status of the
patient. The optimal non HDL-cholesterol level for
intermediate and high risk individuals is less than 2.7
mmol/L. See Can J Cardiol 2013 vol 29 pgs 151 to 167.
Triglyceride <2.30 mmol/L 08/Apr/2026 14:241.36
Hours After Meal h 08/Apr/2026 13:221

During Pregnancy:
T1: 0.10 - 3.50 mU/L
T2: 0.30 - 3.50 mU/L
T3: 0.45 - 4.00 mU/L
Reference intervals are based on treatment thresholds
included in the 2017 American Thyroid Association Guidelines
Autoantibodies
Nuclear Ab <0.7 10/Apr/2026 14:160.1
Antinuclear antibodies, including antibodies to dsDNA or to
extractable nuclear antigens (SS-A, SS-B, Sm, U1RNP, Scl-70,
Jo-1, and Centromere), are not present.
Further testing is not indicated; however, if required,
please contact a Hematopathologist.

Indeterminate:  107-132 pmol/L
Deficient:      <107 pmol/L

levels in the normal range, particularly in elderly
patients.
